title: ProtocolLab WebSocket Performance Contracts
purpose: Define shared deterministic WebSocket message workloads with exact HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, and HTTP/3 binding proof.
scope: The family covers RFC 6455 over HTTP/1.1, RFC 8441 over HTTP/2, and RFC 9220 over HTTP/3. It does not cover WebTransport.

requirements.title: Shared deterministic WebSocket semantics
requirements.statement: WebSocket scenarios MUST declare deterministic text or binary message bytes, message size and count, ordering, masking, fragmentation, control-frame, echo, and close semantics as applicable.

requirements.title: HTTP/1.1 WebSocket upgrade
requirements.statement: HTTP/1.1 WebSocket scenarios MUST prove the RFC 6455 Upgrade handshake, expected endpoint, accepted subprotocol policy, exact HTTP/1.1 binding, and an explicit cleartext ws or TLS-protected wss variant without fallback between them.

requirements.title: HTTP/2 WebSocket extended CONNECT
requirements.statement: HTTP/2 WebSocket scenarios MUST prove RFC 8441 Extended CONNECT with the websocket protocol, SETTINGS enablement, expected endpoint, and exact HTTP/2 binding.

requirements.title: HTTP/3 WebSocket extended CONNECT
requirements.statement: HTTP/3 WebSocket scenarios MUST prove RFC 9220 Extended CONNECT with the websocket protocol, SETTINGS enablement, expected endpoint, and exact HTTP/3 binding.

requirements.title: WebSocket validity gate
requirements.statement: WebSocket measurements MUST validate exact binding, establishment, message type, deterministic bytes or hash, message count, ordering, control-frame behavior when requested, clean close, and zero unexpected failure or timeout outcomes.

requirements.title: No WebSocket binding fallback
requirements.statement: A WebSocket result MUST be invalid when the observed HTTP version or bootstrap mechanism differs from the requested binding.

requirements.title: WebSocket metrics and artifacts
requirements.statement: WebSocket scenarios MUST declare connection-establishment latency, message round-trip latency percentiles, messages and bytes per second, completed, failed, close and timed-out counts, and raw handshake, frame, validation, stdout, stderr, and normalized artifacts.

requirements.title: WebSocket comparison isolation
requirements.statement: WebSocket comparisons MUST match HTTP binding, cleartext or TLS execution variant, subprotocol, message semantics, fragmentation, role, connection reuse, load, network, platform, topology, validation, and repetition policy.

requirements.title: Capability-gated WebSocket breadth diagnostics
requirements.statement: WebSocket diagnostic scenarios that activate ordered multi-message exchange, fragmentation, subprotocol negotiation, or permessage-deflate MUST declare the exact ordering, fragment plan, negotiated token, extension parameters, context-takeover policy, and payload-validation procedure; an executor that lacks the requested capability MUST report unsupported rather than substituting a simpler WebSocket operation.
